_comment: GLOSSARY ENTRY — copy and extend this template for data_glossary.json.
_id: Unique across all glossary entries. Extend the nearest prefix series (see CLAUDE.md). Check for collisions before using.
_target: The Kanien'kéha word or phrase, with full accent marks and length markers.
_english: English meaning. Used for display and as the basis for sortKey (computed — do not add sortKey manually).
_pos: Machine tag. Must be one of the controlled values in CLAUDE.md. Use dot-notation (category.subcategory).
_posLabel: Human-readable display label. May be more specific than pos (e.g. 'Noun — Bird' when pos is 'noun.animal'). Always provide.
_unit_lesson: Zero-based unit index and lesson index where this word is first introduced. Set both to 0 if not yet placed.
notes: Ohón:ta' belongs to the A-class noun paradigm. The plural is the same form — context and number agreement on the verb signal whether one or many are meant. Common in seasonal vocabulary and the Thanksgiving Address.
_notes: Cultural or linguistic context for learners. Explain morphological class, cultural significance, or usage context. Do not just restate the English definition.
_cite: Always include a source. Link to the specific Kontinónhstats lesson page where possible.
_accepts: All strings accepted as correct in typing exercises. Always include: (1) the canonical accented form, (2) the unaccented lowercase form, (3) any dialect variants. Learners are never penalised for their community's orthography.
_forms: Possessive forms, conjugation table, or paradigm. Set to null (not omit — set to null) if no paradigm is needed. Use for kinship terms, noun classes with possessive prefixes, and verbs with conjugation tables.

_comment: MINIMAL VALID ENTRY — only required fields. Use for quick batch additions; expand forms and notes later.
notes: Literally: small leaves of the ground. Common in land and environment vocabulary.
